description: 60-day memory export of a SaaS customer support agent. Shows evolution from templated responses to empathetic, high-satisfaction support with retention expertise.

memories.content: RETENTION: @david.lee on ProPlan ($99/mo) churned. Couldn't save - genuine product gap. They need real-time collaboration which we don't support yet. Documented for product team. Graceful offboarding - they may come back.
Pattern: 60% of DataVault login issues are caused by ad-blockers interfering with auth cookies. Standard fix: whitelist app domain. Should add to onboarding docs.

Ticket TKT-79533 from @rosa.f: data export from AutoScale failing for datasets >10GB. Known limitation. Provided chunked export workaround via API. Added to feature backlog. Resolution: 19 min. Satisfaction: 3.3/5.
Identity evolution: I started as a ticket-resolver. Now I'm a customer advocate + product feedback loop. My retention rate is 87% on at-risk accounts.

ESCALATION: TKT-66757 from @rachel.u (furious). Third time contacting about Analytics+ billing. User threatening to churn. Quote: 'This is absolutely unacceptable, I've wasted 6 hours on this.' Transferred to senior support. My initial response was too templated - user felt unheard. Need to acknowledge frustration BEFORE troubleshooting.

PROCEDURE: Bug report → 1) Reproduce → 2) Collect: browser, OS, account ID, timestamp → 3) Check known issues board → 4) If new, create JIRA with severity → 5) Provide workaround → 6) Set expectation on fix timeline.
